highlights.text: Readers recall that I was looking for a rally to 6744 this week, stating Monday this week: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “My general lean is we correct/consolidate.
Institutions buy these dips the same way everytime: They wait for a scary, violent “elevator down” sell that causes price to take out a major low or shelf of lows.
Every single, violent, “elevator down” sell in ES resolves with an equally violent snap back rally.
Next up, comes the Failed Breakdown and squeeze.

I wrote Tuesday at 4pm: “Another option to long is the Failed Breakdown of today’s daily low at 6693.” Early Wednesday morning we flushed 6693 down to 6680, recovered 6693, then went parabolic to 6770+ by yesterday’s close.

In today’s newsletter, I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into some of the plethora of quality setups we had this week including a monster Failed Breakdown Tuesday (that took us to 6745), a breakdown short late Tuesday (that took us to 6680), then a big Failed Breakdown yesterday (that took us to 6769).

post_title: The First October Dip Was Bought. Is The Parabolic Run Going To Continue In SPX? October 3rd Plan.
